name: Week 2 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Same core lifts — beat your Week 1 numbers. Swap: step-ups replace lunges (different unilateral pattern, easier to control when fatigued). You know your working weights now, so push harder.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front for balance
Bodyweight walking lunges, feel the stretch
W1 working weight: 55lbs plates (100 total). Aim for 4x8 at 55, then try 65 on your last set.
W1 working weight: 65lbs plates (110 total). Aim for 4x10. Slow descent, squeeze glutes at top.
W1 working weight: 360lbs (4 plates/side). Start at 360, aim for 3x12 with full depth.
W1 working weight: 360lbs. Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top. Push toward 20 reps.
W1 working weight: 50lbs. Full ROM, 2-second negative. If 3x12 is easy, bump to 60.
NEW — replaces lunges. DBs at sides, step onto bench. Drive through the top leg, don't push off the back foot. More controlled than lunges when your legs are cooked.

tips: You know your working weights now — no more ramping up. Hit your numbers from set 1.
Squat: warm up with 25 and 35 plates before jumping to working weight
Rest 3 min between squat/RDL sets, 2 min on leg press, 90s on isolation
Step-ups replace lunges — similar muscles, but more stable when you're fried at the end
If the rack is taken, start with leg press — it's just as good for hypertrophy. Hack squat works too.
W1 you were jello by the end. You'll be less wrecked this time — your body is adapting.
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~17 working sets for legs
